science.name: LIGHT WEIGHT
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Physical object (non-biological). ACTIVE MODIFICATION — The Cipher alters the physical makeup of a non-biological object, drastically reducing its weight. Objects affected can shift downward by one to two weight classes, depending on the Cipher's calibration. EFFECT — The object becomes significantly lighter — but only for the user who activated it. Upon picking up or holding the object, the Inquisitor gains limited control over its dimensional properties. As a bonus action, the user can enlarge or shrink the object by up to two size categories in either direction. If an object is reduced beyond its minimum stable size, it defaults to its smallest possible safe configuration. LIMIT: One dimensional adjustment per Bonus Action; calibration holds only while the activating user carries the object.

science.name: MALLIATION
science.effect: Rank: Intern · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Alters the physical properties of the bonded object, allowing it to transition between states of extreme hardness and softness at the Inquisitor's command, its density shifting in response to direct mental or tactile input. EFFECT — The bonded object can be hardened or softened with exceptional precision, ranging from the density of cushioned fabric to the structural integrity of refined diamond. Hard State: resists cutting, crushing, or piercing damage up to Tier-3 materials; may be used offensively (as a weapon of its Tier, standard dice). Soft State: reduces mass and rigidity, absorbs kinetic energy, enables stealth compression. LIMIT: Switching states requires one Reaction or Bonus Action; each full material shift consumes a minor Cipher charge, replenishable upon rest.

science.name: VISIATION
science.effect: Rank: Intern · Component: Animate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Designed for direct neural integration, embedding itself into the user's ocular nerve — a painless bond marked only by a faint geometric shimmer across the eye when active. Once bonded, the Cipher permanently synchronizes with the Inquisitor's visual cortex. EFFECT — For a limited duration, the Inquisitor can perceive the base chemical composition of any object, organism, or substance within their authority level or lower (1 min per charge, standard line of sight). Classified structures above the user's clearance appear redacted. Beyond analytical use, enhances reading comprehension and data absorption. Passive: grants +2 to Perception and Intelligence-related checks reliant on visual or chemical analysis. DURATION: 1 minute per charge · line of sight · redaction is absolute above clearance.

science.name: CHEMICAL ALTER
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, induces a controlled destabilization effect, disrupting the object's molecular cohesion. The object becomes a mutable framework, allowing additional Ciphers to modify or extract its physical attributes. EFFECT — A destabilized object can be deconstructed into its fundamental physical aspects, extracted as tangible particle blocks held in the Inquisitor's hand. Extracted aspects include: Size, Mass, Density, Viscosity, Friction, Force. Each block is a condensed representation of a single property. Two or more blocks can be combined to form composite materials, or applied to existing objects to alter their characteristics. Modified material requires stabilization to prevent further disintegration. KEYWORD — DESTABILIZED: a destabilized object remains mutable until Stabilized. Unattended objects only — matter worn or held by a creature resists destabilization through its bearer.

science.name: STABILIZE
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, generates a molecular lattice that conforms precisely to the object's outer and internal structure, harmonizing molecular bonds and preventing disintegration or further degradation. EFFECT — Stabilizes the target object, halting all destabilization effects and reassembling fragmented materials into a composite whole. Any object previously affected by destabilization becomes perfectly bonded. For damaged or broken items, rebinds all fragments together. Functionality remains limited unless all original components are present — the Cipher preserves form but not missing material.

science.name: SPECTRCO
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Emits low-frequency energy waves across the object's surface, producing a faint, rhythmic luminescent glow that intensifies in synchronization with nearby powered or energetic sources. EFFECT — Upon activation, triggers a light inversion field around anything in direct contact with the host object — brightness becomes shadow, shadow becomes radiant contrast. The object absorbs this inverted light, converting it into a reservoir of condensed electromagnetic energy. Once threshold capacity is reached, the Cipher releases the stored energy as a high-intensity beam of focused electromagnetic waves — a precision discharge or cutting emission dealing 2d10 Radiant (Dexterity Saving Throw for half; against unattended structures it cuts as demolition). CHARGE: threshold capacity ≈ three rounds of absorbed light; unspent charge bleeds off harmlessly at scene's end.

science.name: BF-C
science.effect: Rank: Formal Sciences · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Initiates unpredictable spatial and physical recalibration, causing the host object's position, mass, weight, and density to fluctuate randomly — on both micro and macro scales, with values shifting up to 1,000 times their original measurements. EFFECT — Upon activation, forces the destabilized object to vanish instantaneously, generating a catastrophic butterfly effect that ripple-collapses toward a random point within 1,000 feet of the Inquisitor's location. As the event resolves, the object re-manifests abruptly — typically in the possession or proximity of a random individual within the affected radius, accompanied by a minor energy surge or localized distortion field. LIMIT: Once per scene; the recalibration refuses objects worn or held by a creature.

science.name: RAHMAN-S
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Induces complete optical transparency, dissolving the object's visible form while maintaining full structural integrity — visible only as a faint shimmering distortion under specific light conditions. EFFECT — Manipulates the flow of light through the transparent object, visualizing sound and kinetic vibrations as luminous distortions. Any entity or surface that interacts physically with these vibrations resonates at the same frequency, causing extreme disorientation, vertigo, and sensory instability without inflicting lethal harm. Affected subjects must succeed on: Wisdom Saving Throw (mental stability), Perception Skill Check (visual feedback), Control Skill Check (physical balance). On failure, the subject's body warps and trembles violently in appearance. KEYWORD — DISORIENTED: failed subjects suffer disadvantage on Perception and Action Priority checks until the end of their next turn. Non-lethal by design — the resonance cannot be tuned to wound.

science.name: DIMENSIONAL MAPPING
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Initiates a structural transformation sequence, converting the host into a directional lattice construct — a geometric map of interwoven light and material nodes, shifting in real time to reflect positional awareness. The Curator determines a Dice Value as threshold; the Inquisitor rolls d20 + Ode Level to achieve success. EFFECT — When activated, integrates with the Inquisitor's cognition to project navigational insight toward a previously observed location. The Lattice outlines: most efficient logistical routes to the destination, all notable environmental obstacles and visible dangers, and optimal travel vectors based on terrain, timing, and energy expenditure. Data is displayed through luminous pathways or mental overlays.

science.name: BIPARTITION
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Inanimate Object / Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Generates a small energy node that hovers roughly 6 inches from the object's surface, anchored in place relative to that object. The node appears as a stable point of light or geometric glyph, marking the object as a Bipartite-linked node. The object itself remains fully functional; the node is a purely informational construct. EFFECT — When activated on any linked object, searches for and connects to other objects bearing this same Cipher. A visible line or arc of energy forms between any two linked objects. The Cipher displays the relationship type through line color or pattern (hostile, allied, neutral, resource-linked) and intensity or thickness (stronger or weaker association). As more Ciphered objects exist on the field, the visualization scales — each object becomes a node, each measured relationship an edge, revealing clusters, bridges, or isolated nodes at a glance.

science.name: MASKING
science.effect: Rank: Intern · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When the Cipher is affixed to an object, the object's structure shifts and reforms into a face mask — a smooth, contoured piece that fits over the wearer's face while retaining faint traces of the original material, adjusting its form to match the user's features. EFFECT — When activated while worn, allows the user to comprehend and imitate the thinking patterns and outward behavior of another species they are observing or directly interacting with — intuitively understanding how that species tends to react, move, and communicate. Over time in the same encounter the mask enables the user to mirror their actions or language, reproducing basic speech patterns, tone, and simple phrases closely enough to be treated as a familiar presence rather than an outsider. RESOLUTION: an observer of the imitated species may contest with Insight; behavioral inconsistencies win ties for the observer.

science.name: SYNTHESIS
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Chemical Alter particle blocks (2+). EFFECT — Combines two or more Chemical Alter particle blocks into a new composite material with emergent properties drawn from all source components. Because Online, synthesis data is shared with the Paradigm network — other Science Inquisitors can replicate the composite on future missions. The Paradigm retains the formula on record.

science.name: REFRACTION CASCADE
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Inanimate object (light-interacting). EFFECT — Extends Spectrco's electromagnetic beam by splitting it through the object's internal refraction matrix — fires up to 3 beams simultaneously, each directed at different targets within 60°. Each beam carries reduced power (1d10 Radiant each, Dexterity Saving Throw for half). Beams can be recombined onto a single target for full power. Online upgrade enables directional adjustment mid-activation.

science.name: ISOTOPIC ANALYSIS
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Inanimate object or substance. EFFECT — Reveals the complete material history of a bonded object or substance: chemical composition, isotopic dating (approximate age), origin environment, previous modifications, and last 3 recorded interactions. Data is transmitted to the Paradigm network and archived. Classified materials display redacted fields.

science.name: MOLECULAR ASSEMBLY
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Available base matter. EFFECT — Constructs a functioning object at nanoscale by arranging individual molecules according to a programmed schema. Can create any non-electronic object up to Medium size. Complexity: simple shapes (10 min); functional tools (1 hour); precision instruments (4 hours). Online enables access to Paradigm database assembly schemas for instant pre-approved design fabrication.

science.name: QUANTUM ENTANGLEMENT LINK
science.effect: Rank: Formal Sciences · Component: Two inanimate objects. EFFECT — Establishes quantum entanglement between two bonded objects separated at any distance. Changes made to one object's physical state are reflected in the other regardless of range. Objects must be entangled while adjacent; can then be separated without limit. The link is unbreakable except by Null Deconstruct or Paradigm authority. Online logging records both objects' states in real time.

science.name: PHASE SHIFT DETECTOR
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Inanimate sensor object. EFFECT — Reveals all Phased, metaphysical, simulated, or otherwise non-standard-physical entities within 60 ft — including Remnant Bygone-state subjects, Simulation Parallax entities, Evolution bio-constructs, and Null-abstracted properties. Detected entities appear as a faint overlay within the Inquisitor's vision.

science.name: ATMOSPHERIC CONVERTER
science.effect: Rank: Scientist · Component: Environmental monitoring device. EFFECT — Alters the chemical composition of air within a 40-ft radius: removes specific gases (toxins, smoke, Eldritch particulates), introduces new gases (sedatives, stimulants, oxygen enrichment), or shifts atmospheric density. Creatures in the area make Endurance Saves against gas-based effects. Requires 1 round to reach full effect. Certain gas types require Paradigm authorization. CALIBRATION: the Converter meters every mixture — concentrations never exceed incapacitating thresholds, and lethal compositions refuse authorization at any rank.

science.name: CATALYST
science.effect: Rank: Researcher · Component: Chemical or biological process. EFFECT — Applied to any ongoing chemical or biological process: Accelerate (healing completes in half time; combustion burns at 3× intensity; corrosion that would take hours takes rounds) or Decelerate (stop bleeding; slow poison progression; reduce fire intensity by half). On Genus abilities: extends a sustained effect duration by 50%, or halves a remaining enemy duration. Accelerated processes still resolve through their own listed damage and saves.

science.name: HISTORICAL DATA EXTRACTION
science.effect: Rank: Formal Sciences · Component: Any object. EFFECT — Retrieves a complete chronological record of significant events an object has been part of: every hand that has held it (biological residue), every environment it passed through (particulate deposits), significant forces applied (stress fractures), and approximate timeline. Results are presented as sequential "impressions" — accurate molecular-evidence reconstructions. Classified intel recovered is automatically flagged to the Paradigm.

science.name: S1 — ABSOLUTE ZERO
science.effect: Rank: Distinguished Scientist · Component: Ambient thermal energy. EFFECT — Drops temperature within a 30-ft radius to absolute zero over 2 rounds. All molecular motion ceases — objects become structurally perfect but completely brittle; living creatures make Endurance Saves (DC 18) each round or take 3d10 cold damage and lose 1 Action. Sustained up to 3 rounds. Releasing stored thermal energy detonates it — 5d10 fire damage in the same radius (Dexterity Saving Throw for half). Special Ciphers are never Online-logged. KEYWORD — DEEP FREEZE: the cold incapacitates rather than executes — a creature dropped to 0 HP inside the field is frozen unconscious and stable until thawed.

science.name: S2 — GENESIS FORMULA
science.effect: Rank: Distinguished Scientist · Component: Pure energy source + base matter. EFFECT — Creates real matter from pure energy — up to 50 kg of any non-living material the Inquisitor has previously analyzed via Visiation or Isotopic Analysis. The created matter is indistinguishable from natural material. Cannot create functioning electronics, living organisms, or materials above TL 3.0 in complexity. Created matter is permanent.

science.name: S3 — GRAND UNIFIED THEORY
science.effect: Rank: Formal Sciences Elite · Component: Cognitive integration (self). EFFECT — For 3 rounds, the Inquisitor understands the complete operational logic of any single system they analyze — mechanical, biological, social, or strategic. For combat: gains perfect foreknowledge of the most likely next action of one designated target (pure kinematic prediction, not prophecy). For objects: instantly understands how to use, disable, or replicate any device. RESOLUTION: a target that knows it is being modeled may act against its own logic — deliberate erraticism contests the prediction with Cunning.

science.name: S4 — SYSTEMATIC COLLAPSE
science.effect: Rank: Formal Sciences Elite · Component: Any interconnected system. EFFECT — Triggers a chain reaction of structural failures through any interconnected system the Cipher is bonded to — mechanical systems, facility power grids, or material structures. Collapse propagates at 30 ft per round. Living biology resists cascade bonding — creatures within the collapse radius instead make Endurance Saves (DC 16 + Code Level) each round or take 2d10 damage and have one active ability shut down. Cannot be stopped once initiated except by Null or Paradigm-level authority.

science.name: S5 — LIVING SCIENCE
science.effect: Rank: Distinguished Scientist · Component: Willing living subject. EFFECT — Permanently embeds a scientific Cipher principle into living biology, creating a biological analog of a technology. The effect is slower and imprecise compared to the Cipher but requires no SS (draws from biology instead). Example: Biological Visiation — subject can see chemical compositions naturally. Irreversible without Null ability or major medical intervention.

simulation.name: SIMULATED EJECT
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Simulated Space. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Allows the user to eject a Simulated Element from a Genus Domain in which they possess a chosen Specialty. The Cipher will simulate either the energy type itself (applicable only to Null, Elemental, and Eldritch Energy) or solely the elemental form associated with that domain. The user may also simulate the activation of an Ability from their selected Genus Domain, generating a Simulated Object as the origin point from which the energy or effect is ejected. EFFECT — Ejected abilities apply their effects and deal direct damage consistent with the natural behavior of their associated Genus — the same dice, saves, and limits as the ability they mirror. Once the simulation concludes, all simulated effects immediately cease. Parallax: When Parallaxed, the simulated effect remains active beyond the simulation's end, persisting according to Synaptic Space capacity. For every ten minutes the effect remains active, 10 units of Synaptic Space are consumed. Psychological strain and residual cognitive damage inflicted during this period remain and must be managed separately.

simulation.name: INFLICTION
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Simulation Layer. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied within a simulation, this Cipher manipulates the target's cognitive and emotional equilibrium, embedding an artificial feedback loop between their conscious thought and simulated environment. Upon activation, the target must succeed on an Intelligence Saving Throw against the user's roll and make a Perception Control Skill Check. EFFECT — Failure on either check triggers the Anchored Condition, binding the target's mind to the simulation's emotional construct. This state induces intense emotional backlash — overwhelming the subject with artificially amplified feelings such as fear, grief, or despair. The resulting mental overload causes temporary incapacitation, leaving the target unable to act or process external stimuli until the end of their next turn, or until the simulation resets. LIMIT: Once per target per simulation — a mind that has felt the loop recognizes it.

simulation.name: SIMULATED CLEAVER
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulation Agent · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an object, reshapes its surface and aura to manifest as a static, animated slash of distorted light. Its form shifts subtly depending on the observer's perception — to one a brilliant white arc, to another a fractured rift in space — mirroring the subjective distortions inherent to simulation itself. EFFECT — The modified object can be thrown or projected, and upon impact triggers a Parallax Reaction — either enhancing any active simulation in the surrounding area or forcibly subjecting the struck individual to a localized simulation field. Within this induced state, the target unconsciously confronts a manifestation of their deepest fears. Target must succeed on a Wisdom Saving Throw or be pulled into the generated simulation. Upon escaping, a secondary Wisdom Saving Throw must be made to resist memory suppression — failure results in forgetting they were ever trapped. KEYWORD — SUPPRESSED MEMORY: covers only the time spent inside the induced simulation, and surfaces naturally at the target's next Downtime.

simulation.name: TRACE
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When placed upon an object, generates a simulated lattice of holo-constructive energy that traces the object's surface and internal form, mapping every contour, density layer, and spatial outline with geometric accuracy. EFFECT — The simulated lattice functions as a digital framework of the object, existing simultaneously within tangible and simulated states. This holographic construct can be modified, enhanced, or redefined through accompanying Ciphers — altering material structure, density ratios, or conceptual design without directly modifying the original. Because the lattice is a simulation overlay, it serves as a non-destructive experimental field.

simulation.name: XRYTE
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, compels it to enter a simulated state, converting its form and data into a fully realized simulation construct. If the object is already within a simulation, the Cipher elevates it to a Parallaxed Simulation — merging the simulated and physical planes into tangible illusion and interactive density. EFFECT — When applied within a Parallaxed Simulation, enables the creation of a simulated physical duplicate of an animate subject — modeled after either the Inquisitor or any individual previously imprinted within a Simulated Lattice. The resulting copy possesses the full appearance, reflexes, and material presence of the subject, though its consciousness remains a patterned echo completely obedient to the creating Inquisitor. These duplicates fade upon simulation termination or when Parallax stability is lost. LIMIT: One duplicate sustained at a time; an echo is a pattern, not a person — it carries none of the original's Ciphers, Incepts, or clearances.

simulation.name: DIGITIZE
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Animate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, generates a simulated duplicate within the local simulation field — a perfect digital counterpart complete in form, texture, and behavior. Once duplication completes, the original Cipher transitions fully into the simulation, existing only as code-bound data within the construct. EFFECT — When activated, the Cipher advances or reverses the temporal progression of the simulated object through every stage of its production and existence (as determined by Curator Decreation) — growth, decay, refinement, or restoration at will. A simulated apple could be observed ripening, rotting, or reverting to an unformed seed. The original is never aged — only its forecast.

simulation.name: DISTIE
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Simulated Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, initiates a progressive density reduction sequence, gradually diminishing the object's mass and cohesion over time (Curator Discretion determines rate and degree). The affected object appears distorted or hollow, its edges faintly shimmering as it slips between physical and simulated states. EFFECT — Upon activation, releases a surge of unstable simulation energy that forces any active or nearby simulation to violently collapse in on itself — a shattering implosion of light and energy that wipes out simulated constructs within its range. Physical objects or living entities caught within the destructive radius must succeed on an Endurance Saving Throw — failure results in partial simulation assimilation: the assimilated portion destabilizes with the collapsing environment, dealing 3d10 Force damage and leaving the affected tissue simulation-scarred (visibly pixelated, disadvantage on checks using it) until restored during Downtime.

simulation.name: MANUSIM
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Forces the object into a perfect cubic form, establishing it as a stabilized simulation core — a programmable framework within a Parallax field whose internal lattice becomes malleable to mental or motion-based commands. EFFECT — Allows the cube to reshape itself into complex simulation-based tools or constructs: Projection Tools (cranes, arms, assembly instruments for manipulating large simulated objects), Architectural Constructs (geometric frameworks forming temporary structures or barriers within a running simulation), and Weapon Simulations (bladed, ranged, or field-compressed weapon forms usable in simulated combat, standard weapon dice for their form). Each transformation consumes simulation energy proportional to its complexity.

simulation.name: LITTLE HELPER
simulation.effect: Rank: Sim Intern · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Generates a Simulated Intelligence Construct — a small, semi-holographic humanoid projection often called a "Little Person," its design influenced by the Inquisitor's subconscious preferences or the simulation's environmental template. EFFECT — Once activated, the AI Construct functions as a personal simulation assistant capable of: identifying materials or components necessary for ongoing projects; guiding the Inquisitor through assembly or repair processes step by step; answering inquiries within its programmed knowledge capacity (Paradigm simulations, sciences, and field operations); and highlighting inefficiencies within running simulated sequences. The construct does not possess true sentience, but may develop personality quirks from prolonged interaction.

simulation.name: DISTRUO
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, the object begins to stream data upward — luminous Nodes rising from its surface and stabilizing in the air above it. Each Node is a packet of encoded information, automatically routed to another object bearing a Cipher with the same identification, establishing a persistent data link. EFFECT — When activated, transmits data from one object into another through the Node link. An Intake Node forms near the source object, taking the visual shape of a simulated bowl — any physical object placed into this bowl will be converted into data, stored and sent through the Node chain, and reconstructed inside the direct target object. This transfer can continue from object to object along the chain of linked Ciphers, with no effective distance limit so long as each participating Cipher remains active and connected. Living creatures cannot survive conversion — the Intake refuses them.

simulation.name: SALITE
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulation Agent · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When placed onto an object, the object begins to simulate a focused wavelength that rises straight into the sky, forming an invisible channel between the object and an orbital relay or satellite keyed to this Cipher's signature. Faint distortion, static shimmer, or a soft hum marks the presence of this link. EFFECT — When activated, the satellite connection allows the user to project targeted waves down onto the battlefield, damaging animate subjects within 100 feet by focusing the pulse through their position as a ground anchor (Standard Action per use; 2d8 per pulse, Endurance Saving Throw for half). Through the same link, the user can also transmit their voice across great distances or broadcast it widely. When projecting their presence with intent, those noticeably weaker than the user may feel a pressure like increased gravity pushing them toward the ground — those of comparable strength or strong will can resist this oppressive sensation over time. RESOLUTION: the hum is audible and the object traceable — severing, unkeying, or shielding beneath cover breaks the uplink.

simulation.name: OVERLAY
simulation.effect: Rank: Sim Intern · Component: Inanimate transmitter object. EFFECT — Creates a persistent holographic simulation overlay on a real-world space (up to 30×30 ft). Designated individuals see the overlay; others see normal reality. Can add, remove, or alter visual elements in the covered space. Online: the overlay can receive live updates from Paradigm central simulation servers, enabling real-time intelligence displays or coordinated deception operations.

simulation.name: SIMULATED REINFORCEMENT
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Active simulation or Parallaxed subject. EFFECT — Pulls a simulated entity into a parallel-existence state — it occupies both simulated and real space simultaneously. The entity can interact with real-world objects as if physical, but is immune to non-Parallax damage. Duration: 3 rounds or until the simulation ends. Online enables this to persist past simulation end (Parallax extension), lasting an additional 2 rounds.

simulation.name: NETWORK SIMULATION
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulator · Component: Central simulation node. EFFECT — Establishes a multi-user simulation space (up to 6 Inquisitors). All participants experience the same simulation and can interact with shared elements. Objects created by any participant are visible and usable by all. Online: participants can be at different physical locations within Paradigm network range, allowing remote collaborative operation.

simulation.name: BREACH
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulation Agent · Component: Simulation framework. EFFECT — Forces a simulated entity to manifest physically through a Breach point for 1 round. While breached: the entity is fully physical, deals real damage (its own listed dice), and can be physically damaged. After 1 round, it snaps back to the simulation layer. Online: Breach duration can be extended by 1 round per 10 additional SS spent, up to 4 rounds maximum.

simulation.name: CORRUPTION UPLOAD
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulation Agent · Component: Network-connected device or system. EFFECT — Injects simulated malware into any networked device or digital system. The corruption: disables one chosen function per round; spreads through connected systems at 1 additional system per 2 rounds; causes false sensor data to be generated. Life-critical systems (medical suites, life support) degrade to safe-mode rather than shutdown. Because Online, the corruption signature is traceable — unauthorized use is a logged violation with automatic mission review.

simulation.name: REALITY ANCHOR
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulation Agent · Component: Active Parallax effect. EFFECT — Permanently sustains one designated Parallax effect without requiring continued SS investment. The anchored effect is stable until physically disrupted or the Inquisitor deactivates it. Because Online: the Paradigm can remotely deactivate Anchored effects. One Anchor can be maintained per Cipher use.

simulation.name: MIRROR WORLD
simulation.effect: Rank: Formal Simulations · Component: Current environment (30-ft radius). EFFECT — Creates a simulation that is a perfect real-time copy of the current physical environment. Changes in the real environment are reflected in the simulation and vice versa. Inquisitors can step into and out of the simulation as a free action. Combat in the Mirror World deals real damage (via Parallax, through each attack's own listed dice). Mirror World persists for 5 minutes.

simulation.name: SIGNAL INTERCEPT
simulation.effect: Rank: Simulation Agent · Component: Active simulation network. EFFECT — Taps into any active Parallax or Simulation Cipher in range (60 ft). Can observe the simulation's contents from outside, inject a single simulated element into the hijacked simulation, or redirect Parallax effects to a new target. Contest: d20 + Code Level vs the originating Inquisitor's DC. On failure: originating Inquisitor is alerted to the intercept attempt.

simulation.name: COMPLETE OVERRIDE
simulation.effect: Rank: Formal Simulations · Component: Target's perception system. EFFECT — Replaces all of a target's sensory input with a complete fabricated simulation environment (Wisdom Save DC 16). The target experiences a fully immersive simulation replacing all their senses — they cannot perceive the real world. Duration: until target succeeds a Wisdom Save (retries each round at +1 cumulative bonus), until the Inquisitor is incapacitated, or until physically shaken out of it (Standard Action by adjacent ally). KEYWORD — IMMERSED: an Immersed target's body idles defensively — the fabricated environment cannot walk it into hazards, and real damage taken grants an immediate bonus re-Save.

simulation.name: S1 — PERSISTENT WORLD
simulation.effect: Rank: Distinguished Simulator · Component: Dedicated Paradigm hardware. EFFECT — Creates a simulation that exists and evolves independently — accessible at any time by authorized users. The Persistent World retains all elements created within it across sessions. It has a continuous Parallax status. The Inquisitor is the World's administrator but cannot control its evolution after creation. Excommunication results in immediate World deletion.

simulation.name: S2 — PARALLAX COLLAPSE
simulation.effect: Rank: Distinguished Simulator · Component: Network broadcast transmitter. EFFECT — Broadcasts a catastrophic destabilization signal causing ALL active simulations and Parallax effects within 1 mile to collapse simultaneously and violently. Creatures within Parallaxed simulations when they collapse take 3d10 force damage (no Save). All Simulation Ciphers in range are forced offline for 1d4 rounds. Indiscriminate — affects allied and enemy simulations equally. Requires Paradigm mission authorization.

simulation.name: S3 — TRUE SIMULATION
simulation.effect: Rank: Distinguished Simulator · Component: Full environment. EFFECT — Creates a simulation so complete in physical, chemical, and biological fidelity that it is indistinguishable from reality by any measurable standard — including supernatural analysis. Everything within obeys physics perfectly. Null Deconstruct cannot distinguish its matter from real matter. Duration: 1 hour per Code Level. Entities created within it believe themselves real. The Inquisitor and up to 5 designated individuals know the distinction.

simulation.name: S4 — VOID UPLOAD
simulation.effect: Rank: Distinguished Simulator · Component: Subject's consciousness-interface hardware. EFFECT — Transfers a living consciousness into a pure simulation state — the body becomes a blank biological shell and the mind enters the Paradigm simulation network as a digital entity. While uploaded: immune to physical harm but can be affected by Cipher-level simulation attacks. The vacated body is kept in autonomic stasis for the duration. Return requires re-download (15 min); an interrupted download simply resumes from the network copy — the mind is buffered, never lost.

simulation.name: S5 — THE FINAL DRAFT
simulation.effect: Rank: Distinguished Simulator Elite · Component: Paradigm historical archive access. EFFECT — Retroactively designates one completed event (within the last 24 hours, not exceeding a single encounter in scope) as simulated — it didn't happen. Evidence is adjusted, memories of non-resistant witnesses are overwritten (Wisdom Save DC 19 to retain true memory), and physical consequences are reversed. Cannot undo death, and cannot cause it — a redraft that would leave anyone deceased who wasn't refuses to compile. Cannot undo Director-designated permanent narrative events. The most tightly regulated Cipher in the Simulation suite.

remnant.name: MULTITOOL
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Physical Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Allows the attached Cipher to transform a chosen object into one of five adaptive forms. Available Forms: Screwdriver (enhances precision during tinkering/repair), Wrench (increased mechanical leverage for machinery), Mini Laser Blade (compact high-intensity energy edge for cutting; as a weapon, 1d8 Fire), Distance Enhancer (projects a simulated reconstruction of a distant object within 3 feet for full visual/spatial analysis), EMP Module (controlled pulse to disable automated systems, excluding cybernetic/cyborg-level processors). EFFECT — Upon activation, the Cipher permanently links to the object it modifies, altering its internal structure to accommodate modular transformation. Each form functions autonomously, drawing on the user's energy or environmental reserves as needed. The Remnant Paradigm often equips field operatives with this modification due to its tactical flexibility.

remnant.name: SPYDER
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When embedded into an object, this Cipher grants it animated properties, awakening a limited, synthetic form of motion and intent. The object becomes partially autonomous, capable of executing simple actions in response to the Inquisitor's commands or instinctual needs, gaining the ability to apply Intrusion. EFFECT — The animated entity actively assists its wielder, performing supportive or defensive tasks. It has the unique ability to imbed itself into any Inanimate Object or Animate Subject. Once embedded, it establishes a direct Causal Connection with any active Cipher or Genus ability. Delay Activation: requires a DC 20 Control check — on a success, the activation of the connected Cipher or Genus is suspended and set to trigger automatically at any designated moment within a 1-Hour (ITT) window. Forceful Activation: converts a failed roll associated with the connected capability into a Minimum Success; this forced resolution is delayed, executing immediately after the player's current turn ends in the Action Priority stack. Reactivation: bypasses standard cooling protocols to instantly reactivate a Cipher expended up to 2 turns prior. LIMIT: One Causal Connection held at a time; each function (Delay, Forceful, Reactivation) cycles once per encounter. Embedding into an Animate Subject is felt — an unwilling host may contest with an Adaptation check.

remnant.name: QUICK HACK
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, the object itself — not the Cipher — begins broadcasting a Signal. This Signal can be interacted with through other Ciphers, but never by external technology unless that technology has already been altered by your own Ciphers. EFFECT — The user opens an empty decoy cipher to "public" domains, allowing it to scan all technological signals within a 200-ft range. Decoy: anything that attempts to breach the decoy is contained within it and flagged by the Inquisitor's system — it cannot reach the rest of the system from the decoy, and is effectively trapped. Seizure: should the decoy remain safe, it can be handed the functional portion of the Cipher, connecting to a single public domain and taking seizure of all passive systems within — motion detectors, cameras, and the like — along with their recorded and logged information. Passive infrastructure only: autonomous units, robots, and anything that acts under its own direction cannot be Quick Hacked. Logged information can be copied to the Paradigm system or erased from the enemy system — always held in the decoy first to confirm nothing malicious rides along, then sent to Inquisitors. LIMIT: One decoy active and one public domain seized at a time.

remnant.name: PASTING VIEW
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, this Cipher automatically migrates to the ocular nerve, embedding within the eye — a painless integration leaving a subtle luminescent ring within the iris that activates only when in use. EFFECT — While integrated, grants the Inquisitor the ability to visually reconstruct past events within a target area. By focusing their vision on a location, the user can perceive what transpired there within the previous hour. This reconstruction manifests as ghostlike, translucent echoes of action — motion and behavior rendered clearly, though individual faces, identifiers, and fine details remain indistinct.

remnant.name: PHASE
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to a living subject, the Cipher attaches to the spinal column, integrating with the nervous system — externally a small reflective chip at the base of the neck, pulsing faintly in rhythm with the subject's energy signature. EFFECT — While active, grants the subject the ability to exude concentrated energy that manifests visually as afterimages shaped in the Inquisitor's own likeness. These energy projections mimic the user's movements with slight delay or echo, producing ghost-like trails that remain briefly visible before fading. The intensity, color, and clarity of these afterimages correspond directly to the user's energy output and emotional state — allowing for strategic visual manipulation, obscuring movement, disorienting opponents, or emphasizing command presence.

remnant.name: GLIMPSE
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, creates a localized friction-reduction field, subtly diminishing surface resistance and sound generation — smoother in movement, quieter when handled, almost no physical drag or audible trace. EFFECT — Upon activation, the Cipher extends its influence to the Inquisitor. While active: the Inquisitor gains advantage on all Perception Skill Checks and Dexterity Saving Throws. When about to be observed by an animate subject, the Inquisitor may roll a d20 + Ode Level, contested against the target's Perception Skill Check (modified by a secondary factor at Curator Discretion). On success, the Inquisitor avoids detection entirely — their movement and signature rendered imperceptible by both sight and sound.

remnant.name: TACHYEON
remnant.effect: Rank: Contractor · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, induces a subtle vibrational flux — a near-inaudible hum and faint kinetic shimmer synced to the Inquisitor's neural signature, aligning their reflexive timing with the Cipher's micro-frequency field. EFFECT — Heightens neuromuscular reaction time. When the Inquisitor would gain advantage on a Dexterity-based roll following Action Priority resolution, they may trigger the Cipher's effect to attempt a three-part precision challenge — the opponent contests: Perception Check, Precision Skill Check, and Inspiration Skill Check against the user's DCs. If the Inquisitor wins two out of three checks, they momentarily displace their position, warping behind the target (not teleportation, but a simulated temporal realignment). Upon successful repositioning, the Inquisitor gains a True Opportunity Attack that automatically registers as a Critical Success — resolving through the weapon's own dice and the standard critical rules. LIMIT: Once per combat sequence.

remnant.name: VECRAVEL
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, causes the item to partially submerge into the terrain beneath it without breaking cohesion or stability — absorbed slowly into the surface tension of the ground, suspended between physical and sub-physical states until activation. EFFECT — Upon activation, the object fully phases beneath the ground and reemerges within 30 feet of the Inquisitor's position, bursting upward from the surface in a controlled, localized displacement. The transition obeys spatial geometry but ignores line of sight, allowing the object to travel through solid terrain, material obstacles, or environmental interference without resistance. The object's reappearance is accompanied by a subtle gravitational ripple and displacement shockwave — enough to disturb light debris but not cause direct harm.

remnant.name: TIGHTEN
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, instills a latent compression field within its structure. If altered, damaged, or reshaped in any way, the object begins to constrict upon itself, the material tightening as though wrapped by invisible threads of high-tension wire — silent but deliberate, with faint creaking as it resists further alteration. EFFECT — The object's frame contracts violently and folds inward, twisting until it crushes its own mass into an unusable fragment. Any external material attached to or dependent on the object is also pulled inward, rendering the target nonfunctional and irrecoverable without advanced restorative procedures. The process causes no explosive release or shrapnel — only a controlled collapse, as though the object implodes into itself and ceases to exist in its usable form. KEYWORD — COMPRESSION FIELD: binds to unattended objects only; matter worn or held by a creature resists the field through its bearer, and the collapse releases anything living before it closes.

remnant.name: INCOGNITO
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, the object begins to visually imitate the materials in its immediate surroundings, taking on the color, texture, and general appearance of whatever exists within a 5-foot radius — stone echoing stone, metal echoing metal, mixed environments producing blended camouflage. EFFECT — When activated, the object melds with the environment, becoming difficult to distinguish from the terrain or structures it is adjacent to. Anything directly touching the object inherits those same visual properties, effectively pulled into the camouflage field. If a creature attempts to detect or see through this concealment, they must succeed on either an Inspiration or Perception Skill Check (Curator's choice) against the set DC.

remnant.name: BYGONE
remnant.effect: Rank: Chosen / Specialist · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, that object ceases to exist in the physical state and is converted into a stable metaphysical construct, sustained entirely as organized energy rather than matter. In this form it cannot be touched, moved, or affected by normal physical means, and can only be perceived or manipulated through technology or abilities specifically designed to interact with metaphysical constructs. Its internal atomic and nucleic structure remains perfectly intact and preserved within the energetic pattern. EFFECT — When activated, anything directly touching the object at that moment becomes temporarily influenced by its properties, shifting into a similar metaphysical state — only able to be seen, affected, and interacted with by metaphysical constructs or compatible technologies. While in this state, an animate subject gains advantage on all Perception, Control, and Dexterity checks, and their movement speed is effectively doubled (moving as if gliding yet still subject to gravity's general pull). They may also roll a d20 plus their Ode level to determine whether they can pass through a chosen solid object — the exact manner and limitations determined jointly by the Object's nature and Curator interpretation. A metaphysical subject cannot deal physical-state damage until they rematerialize.

remnant.name: ECHO CHAIN
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Inanimate object. EFFECT — Projects up to 4 independent Phase echoes at separate locations within 30 ft — each a convincing duplicate of the Inquisitor. Echoes persist for 2 rounds. Attackers must succeed a Perception check (DC 13) to identify the real Inquisitor. Online: echoes receive live data from Paradigm positioning satellites, allowing them to mimic predictive behavior rather than simply standing idle.

remnant.name: MEMORY LOCK
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Animate (self). EFFECT — Crystallizes one observed past event (from a Pasting View activation) as a perfectly accurate permanent memory — accessible at any time with full sensory detail. Without Memory Lock, Pasting View impressions fade within hours. Online: memory can be uploaded to Paradigm archives and shared with authorized Inquisitors as an official evidence record.

remnant.name: TEMPORAL FOLD
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Temporal echo anchor. EFFECT — The Inquisitor briefly perceives and can interact with the physical state of the current location 10 seconds ago — objects that were moved are visible in previous positions; creatures who have since left are visible as ghosts. Limited physical interaction is possible. Duration: 1 round of access. Online: extendable to 3 rounds with Paradigm temporal support.

remnant.name: GHOST PROTOCOL
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Environmental material. EFFECT — The Inquisitor and up to 3 allies within 10 ft simultaneously meld with the current environment. All affected individuals become visually indistinguishable from the surroundings and inherit Incognito's detection requirements. Moving at more than half speed breaks individual concealment.

remnant.name: REMNANT RETRIEVAL
remnant.effect: Rank: Contractor · Component: Destroyed object's remnant echo. EFFECT — If an object has been destroyed within the last 1 hour, its Remnant echo persists in the location of its destruction. This Cipher pulls the echo back into physical reality — the object is reconstructed to its pre-destruction state. Material integrity: 50% structural integrity unless a Precision check (DC 15) is made for full restoration. Cannot retrieve living beings.

remnant.name: SPECTRAL LINK
remnant.effect: Rank: Contractor · Component: Fallen Inquisitor's last active Cipher. EFFECT — If an Inquisitor has fallen (0 HP) within the last 3 rounds and within 60 ft, reads the last ability the fallen Inquisitor had active and recreates it once at half SS cost, using the living Inquisitor as the origin point. The recreated ability uses the fallen Inquisitor's stats for effect calculations.

remnant.name: PHASE CASCADE
remnant.effect: Rank: Dependent · Component: Multiple inanimate objects (up to 5). EFFECT — Phases up to 5 bonded objects beneath ground simultaneously. All 5 can emerge at different points within the Inquisitor's movement range this turn (up to 30 ft each from burial point). Each object emerges at a designated point — enabling coordinated deployments: simultaneous barrier placement, multi-point ambush setup, or distributed equipment drop.

remnant.name: ADAPTIVE CAMOUFLAGE
remnant.effect: Rank: Wanderer · Component: Environmental sensor suite (self). EFFECT — Unlike standard Incognito (static environment meld), Adaptive Camouflage updates in real time — the Inquisitor's concealment adjusts as they move through different environments. Transition between environments does not break concealment. Moving at full speed is allowed without breaking effect. Costs 2 SS per minute maintained.

remnant.name: PERFECT RECALL
remnant.effect: Rank: Chosen / Specialist · Component: Animate (self). EFFECT — Access any past event the Inquisitor was personally present for with complete and accurate sensory fidelity — sight, sound, smell, temperature, and proprioception. Unlike ordinary memory (which is reconstructive and imperfect), Perfect Recall is a Remnant-echo playback — literally accessing the echo of the past self's experience. Can be viewed while still acting normally.

remnant.name: S1 — THE REMNANT
remnant.effect: Rank: Distinguished Operative · Component: Self. EFFECT — The Inquisitor fully converts to an energy echo of themselves — a Remnant state — for up to 1 hour. Fully immune to physical damage; can pass through solid matter; can interact with objects in Phase or Bygone states. Retains full cognitive function and can use all Remnant Ciphers. Cannot deal physical damage — can only affect Phased or Remnant-state targets. Returning to physical form requires 1 full round.

remnant.name: S2 — CHRONAL ECHO
remnant.effect: Rank: Distinguished Operative · Component: Target location (enclosed space). EFFECT — Accesses and partially interacts with the complete Remnant history of one location — every significant event that occurred there leaves an echo. The Inquisitor perceives all echoes simultaneously, layered. Can selectively focus on specific time periods. Physical interaction with historical echoes is possible — extracting information or retrieving objects that "existed" in the location at a past point. Duration: 10 minutes.

remnant.name: S3 — ABSOLUTE INCOGNITO
remnant.effect: Rank: Distinguished Operative · Component: Self. EFFECT — The Inquisitor becomes completely undetectable by any means — physical, supernatural, technological, and perceptual — for 10 minutes. Cannot be revealed by Divination, detected by Ciphers, tracked by Paradigm network, or perceived by any Genus ability. Even their Paradigm cannot know their location while active. Ends instantly on any hostile action or ability activation.

remnant.name: S4 — LEGACY CIPHER
remnant.effect: Rank: Distinguished Operative · Component: Location (must remain). EFFECT — Permanently embeds a single Cipher effect into a physical location as an environmental trigger. Any creature who triggers the designated condition activates the embedded effect — at full original power, resolving through the embedded Cipher's own checks and dice. The Legacy Cipher persists indefinitely until triggered, or until Null-deconstructed. Can only embed Offline Cipher effects.

remnant.name: S5 — THE LAST STAND
remnant.effect: Rank: Distinguished Operative · Component: Self (passive). EFFECT — Passive Cipher that activates automatically when the Inquisitor is reduced to exactly 0 HP. For 1 additional round, the Inquisitor continues acting as a full Remnant echo — they can take a complete turn (all Actions and Reactions) as if at full capacity. At the end of this echo round, they fall normally. This Cipher cannot be deactivated, traded, or modified — once bonded, it is permanent.

cognition.name: MIND TWEAK
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, the Cipher embeds itself into the target's cognitive signal pathways, establishing a passive monitoring link. From this point, the Inquisitor can read emotional flux and sense surface-level intention without triggering active psychic detection. EFFECT — The Inquisitor gains the ability to subtly influence the target's thinking process — amplifying doubt, suppressing aggression, or nudging a decision toward a preferred outcome. These alterations are small and non-invasive, undetectable by normal means. Larger modifications require an opposed Wisdom Saving Throw. On failure, the target complies with one minor suggestion before recovering. On Critical Success against their will, the target believes the influenced thought originated from themselves. SURGEON'S RULE: suggestions that would drive the subject to harm itself or betray its core identity collapse on contact — the mind's own architecture rejects them.

cognition.name: DETECTION
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, the Cipher synchronizes with the object's material composition and electromagnetic signature, allowing it to detect incoming signals, vibrations, or cognitive emissions within range. EFFECT — The Inquisitor becomes aware of any cognition-based Cipher or mental ability being used within the object's detection radius. Upon detection, the Inquisitor can identify the ability type, the approximate origin point, and whether the emitter is aware of being detected. They may then attempt to trace the cognitive link back to its source.

cognition.name: BRAIN WASH
cognition.effect: Rank: Auditor · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, establishes deep cognitive contact. The Cipher lowers the target's mental resistance through repeated micro-pulses over the activation period, gradually softening the structural integrity of their memory architecture. EFFECT — The Inquisitor can selectively erase, overwrite, or implant specific memories within the target's conscious recall — one memory block per activation. Implanted memories feel authentic. Erased memories leave no awareness of absence. Target must succeed on a contested Wisdom Saving Throw; additional Insight Checks from outside parties may identify inconsistencies. On Critical Failure, the target's entire recent memory block is compromised, leaving the subject temporarily amnesiac and highly suggestible. SURGEON'S RULE: structural identity — name, bonds, core self — resists the pulses; a mind stripped of a block rebuilds toward its baseline over the following Downtime unless re-washed.

cognition.name: TRANSLATE
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to the user, temporarily expands the neural processing layer associated with language comprehension, pulling in real-time linguistic pattern data from cognitive contact with the target environment or entity. EFFECT — Allows the Inquisitor to understand and speak any language encountered, including constructed, encoded, or non-verbal communication systems. Extends to gestural languages, tonally-dependent dialects, and symbolic script systems the Inquisitor observes. Duration scales with Ode Level. Languages encountered during active use are catalogued and accessible in future sessions at reduced Synaptic Space cost.

cognition.name: FILTER
cognition.effect: Rank: Empath · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, establishes a selective cognitive barrier at the threshold of conscious thought, intercepting incoming emotional or psychic transmissions before they can be registered by the host consciousness. EFFECT — Provides immunity to emotional manipulation, psychic intrusion, and cognitive assault for the duration. Hostile cognition-based Ciphers targeting the protected subject automatically fail unless the attacker's roll exceeds the Filter's established threshold by 10 or more. Also grants resistance against Fear, Charm, and Confusion conditions. Can be applied to other targets, not solely the Inquisitor themselves.

cognition.name: PERCEPTI
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to the Inquisitor, dramatically expands their active sensory bandwidth. Visual, auditory, and environmental stimuli are processed in enhanced detail, each signal broken down into layered data streams before being reconstructed into a comprehensive situational awareness field. EFFECT — The Inquisitor perceives the environment through an overlaid cognition grid — a data-dense projection layered over standard sight that highlights: cognitive signatures of nearby animate beings (intent, emotional state, projected movement), structural weak points or invisible features in the environment, and inconsistencies in simulated or altered spaces. Passive: grants +3 to all Perception-based checks while active.

cognition.name: ARITHMETIC
cognition.effect: Rank: Empath · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, generates a cognitive thread linking their logical processing centers to an external framework driven by the Inquisitor's own mental architecture — remote influence over how the target weighs data, makes decisions, and resolves equations. EFFECT — The Inquisitor can inject logical constructs — inescapable paradoxes, self-contradicting belief sequences, or mathematical impossibilities — directly into the target's decision-making process. This causes the target to become locked in involuntary analytical loops, unable to act until their cognition resolves the injected construct (Curator determines resolution time). Particularly complex injections can subtly alter the target's priority hierarchy — unnoticed until a critical moment, and unwinding naturally at the target's next Downtime. RESOLUTION: a mind that resolves a construct is inoculated — immune to further injections from the same Inquisitor for the rest of the scene.

cognition.name: DIFFUSE
cognition.effect: Rank: Empath · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, causes it to emit a field of diffuse cognitive interference — low-level white noise targeting the neural signal layer of all animate beings within range, not strong enough to cause harm but sufficient to muddy perception and reaction clarity. EFFECT — All animate subjects within the interference radius (excluding the Inquisitor and chosen allies) suffer disadvantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Perception-based checks while inside the field. Complex decision-making slows. Cognitive Ciphers used by affected subjects have their effectiveness reduced. The field is invisible and non-threatening in appearance — targets may not immediately identify the source of their deteriorating cognition.

cognition.name: ALGORIM
cognition.effect: Rank: Empath · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, embeds a predictive cognitive algorithm deep within their behavioral response matrix. This algorithm silently catalogs patterns — reaction times, decision tendencies, emotional triggers, and combat rhythms — building a behavioral model unique to the target. EFFECT — The Inquisitor can access this behavioral model to anticipate the target's next action. Once per encounter, the Inquisitor may declare a Predicted Action before the target takes their turn. If the declaration is correct, the Inquisitor may immediately respond as if they had already prepared that countermeasure — gaining automatic advantage on all checks against that action. The algorithm grows more accurate with extended exposure, improving from 50% match probability at first use to near-certainty after three successful encounters with the same subject.

cognition.name: RESONANT FIELD
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate (self or target). ACTIVE MODIFICATION — Establishes a localized cognitive resonance field that amplifies existing mental states of all animate subjects within range. EFFECT — Within a 20-ft radius, all currently active mental states (focus, aggression, calm, fear) are amplified to their maximum intensity. Focused creatures gain advantage on their next INT-based action; aggressive creatures gain +2 damage but −1 to defensive checks; frightened creatures' Fear condition escalates. Duration: 2 rounds.

cognition.name: NEURAL BRIDGE
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate (self + ally). EFFECT — Both Inquisitors share a real-time tactical mental feed — each knows the other's current Perception data, HP approximation, active conditions, and immediate intentions. Shared SS pools: Inquisitors can loan SS to each other through the Bridge (up to 10 SS per round transfer). Duration: entire encounter or until one party breaks the link.

cognition.name: MEMORY WIPE
cognition.effect: Rank: Auditor · Component: Animate target. EFFECT — Following a successful Brain Wash (or standalone against unconscious targets), selectively erases specific memories from the target's accessible memory. The erasure is accurate — no false memories replace the gaps, just absence. Structural identity remains untouchable under the Surgeon's Rule. Online: Paradigm archives a record of all wiped content for accountability.

cognition.name: COGNITIVE DOWNLOAD
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate target (willing or incapacitated). EFFECT — Copies a specific area of expertise or information set from the target directly into the user's accessible memory — languages, technical knowledge, tactical information, codes, maps. The download is instantaneous — the Inquisitor gains the target's level of expertise in the designated domain for 24 hours. Target retains their knowledge — it is copied, not removed.

cognition.name: MASS DETECTION
cognition.effect: Rank: Seeker · Component: Animate (self). EFFECT — Extends Detection to a 30-ft radius affecting all creatures simultaneously. The Inquisitor receives continuous social/moral guidance cues for every creature in range. Advantage on all social skill checks and group management decisions. At rank Auditor and above: can selectively isolate and intensify the signal on one target.

cognition.name: PSYCHIC SCREAM
cognition.effect: Rank: Auditor · Component: Animate (self). EFFECT — All creatures in a 35-ft radius make Wisdom Saves (DC 14 + Neuronal Capacity Modifier) or take 2d8 psychic damage and are Stunned for 1 round. On success: half damage, not Stunned. The Inquisitor takes 1d4 psychic backlash damage regardless. Allied Cognition Inquisitors can be designated immune with a Bonus Action setup.

cognition.name: DREAM STATE
cognition.effect: Rank: Auditor · Component: Animate target. EFFECT — Target (Wisdom Save DC 13) enters a semi-aware state: they believe they are experiencing a vivid dream. They continue basic functions but cannot make complex decisions, cannot use abilities requiring Intent, and perceive their surroundings with surreal distortion. The dreaming mind still guards its body — it drifts away from obvious hazards. They will fail the first Roll that they are given, then be brought out of the state if targeted by anything that requires a roll. Duration: until the target takes damage or succeeds a re-Save (retry each round at +1 cumulative bonus).

cognition.name: COGNITIVE FORTRESS
cognition.effect: Rank: Auditor · Component: Animate (self + allies within 15 ft). EFFECT — For 2 rounds, the Inquisitor and all allies within 15 ft gain immunity to all mind-affecting abilities, fear effects, Brain Wash variants, Algorim, and related effects. Existing mental conditions are suppressed (not removed) for the duration. Online: Paradigm can remotely reinforce Cognitive Fortresses for Inquisitors on critical missions (extends duration to 4 rounds).

cognition.name: SYNAPTIC SURGE
cognition.effect: Rank: Empath · Component: Animate (target). EFFECT — Target's maximum Synaptic Space is increased by 60 × Rank Multiplier for the duration of one encounter. After the encounter ends, any SS above normal maximum is drained, and the target makes an Endurance Save (DC 12) or takes 1d6 psychic damage from the neural strain. Can be self-applied.

cognition.name: COLLECTIVE MIND
cognition.effect: Rank: Empath · Component: Network of up to 6 willing Cognition Inquisitors. EFFECT — Up to 6 willing Cognition Inquisitors pool their Synaptic Space into a shared collective bank. Any participant can draw from the collective pool for their abilities. The total is the sum of all participants' current SS. Damage to any participant reduces the collective pool proportionally. Online: requires Paradigm server synchronization — full oversight of expenditure.

cognition.name: S1 — OMNISCIENT MOMENT
cognition.effect: Rank: Distinguished Cognitioneer · Component: Self. EFFECT — For exactly 1 round, the Inquisitor knows: the precise intended action of every creature within 60 ft before that creature has taken it; the exact current HP and active conditions of all creatures in range; and the optimal defensive and offensive decision for this round. This is not prophecy — it is extreme predictive modeling at Cipher fidelity. After the Omniscient Moment ends: Downtime for 1 round.

cognition.name: S2 — SOUL STITCH
cognition.effect: Rank: Distinguished Cognitioneer · Component: Two willing subjects. EFFECT — Creates a permanent cognitive bond between two willing subjects. Both are always aware of: the other's physical location (accurate to 30 ft); their current emotional state; and whether they are conscious. While bonded: both gain +1 to all checks when within 60 ft of each other. The Stitch is permanent and does not require SS to maintain.

cognition.name: S3 — THE EMPTY
cognition.effect: Rank: Distinguished Cognitioneer · Component: Target creature. EFFECT — Target (Wisdom Save DC 18) has their personality, will, and memory temporarily suspended — leaving a blank, obedient biological shell. The Empty subject: follows any direct instruction its body can survive — the autonomic core is the one thing suspension cannot reach, and orders that would end or mutilate the shell simply halt it in place; retains full physical capability; has no emotional or moral capacity; does not retain memory of their time Empty. Duration: 1 hour. Paradigm considers unauthorized use one of its most serious ethical violations.

cognition.name: S4 — COGNITIVE CASCADE
cognition.effect: Rank: Distinguished Cognitioneer · Component: Multiple animate targets. EFFECT — Algorim chains through up to 4 targets simultaneously — the Inquisitor puppets all of them in real time. Each target's movements are individually controllable. Targets resist with Wisdom Saves (DC 15 + Neuronal Capacity Modifier) each round. A puppet's self-preservation cannot be overridden — a directive that would destroy the puppet snaps the chain for that target. While Cascade is active: the Inquisitor cannot move but can channel all of their abilities through any of the Cascaded subjects. The cognitive strain deals 1d6 psychic damage to the Inquisitor per active puppet per round.

cognition.name: S5 — ENLIGHTENMENT
cognition.effect: Rank: Distinguished Cognitioneer · Component: One mystery, object, situation, or entity. EFFECT — The Inquisitor understands the complete truth of the designated subject with absolute clarity — no ambiguity, no deception, no incomplete information. For an object: its full history, composition, and purpose. For a situation: its causes, current state, and most likely consequences. For an entity: their true nature, motivations, and all known capabilities. For a mystery: the accurate answer. Director confirms accuracy.

evolution.name: GENETIC MODEL
evolution.effect: Rank: Tester · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, scans the subject's full genetic architecture and renders it as a precise data model stored within the Cipher's memory — a complete biological blueprint including species markers, trait expressions, latent mutations, and structural anomalies. EFFECT — The Inquisitor can reference and share this model at any time. It functions as a baseline against which all future modifications are measured. If modifications are applied to the subject without a stored Genetic Model, reverting to the original form becomes significantly more difficult or impossible. Models can be transmitted to other Inquisitors with compatible Evolution Ciphers, enabling coordinated biological operations.

evolution.name: GENE EDIT
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject (with or without an existing Genetic Model), performs a direct edit to one or more genetic sequences. The scope and complexity of the edit are determined by the Inquisitor's Ode Level and Modificator rank, as well as the Curator's assessment of biological plausibility. EFFECT — Allows precise permanent or temporary modifications to a subject's biology: enhancing strength, speed, or endurance; suppressing or activating dormant traits; removing inherited conditions; or introducing synthetic sequences. Modifications without a Genetic Model risk unpredictable cascades — the Curator rolls secretly to determine whether side effects emerge. With a stored Model, precision is guaranteed within the bounds of the Cipher's scope. RESOLUTION: unwilling subjects resist with an Endurance Saving Throw; edits never sever vital function — biology under the knife keeps its vital floor.

evolution.name: MODIFY
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, induces a rapid but controlled morphological shift. The modification targets phenotypic expression rather than the underlying sequence — altering visible or functional traits in real time without permanently rewriting the genetic foundation. EFFECT — The subject undergoes a temporary change to their physical form: shifting size, altering skin density, extending limb proportions, modifying surface texture, or adjusting metabolic rate. Duration is based on Ode Level. On expiration, the subject returns to their baseline unless a Gene Edit was applied to lock the modification in place. Repeated use on the same subject without rest can cause trait fatigue — diminishing returns on modification potency. RESOLUTION: unwilling subjects resist with an Endurance Saving Throw.

evolution.name: DEVELOP
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, triggers a precise acceleration of cellular development in a targeted tissue group or organ system. The affected region undergoes compressed growth and functional maturation far beyond natural timelines. EFFECT — The targeted area heals, regenerates, or grows at accelerated rate. Wounds close over one to three rounds depending on severity. Damaged organs recover partial or full function. Atrophied systems regain strength. Can also be used to mature developing or immature tissue — enabling rapid physical growth in a controlled region. Excessive use without recovery time risks overgrowth or cellular instability in the affected area.

evolution.name: INDUCE
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, introduces a synthetic or natural biochemical compound directly into the target's biological system, bypassing standard absorption barriers and interacting directly with cellular receptors. EFFECT — The Inquisitor selects one of three induction types: Stimulant (heightens physical performance, grants temporary bonus to Strength, Speed, and Endurance-based checks), Suppressant (dampens metabolic activity, reducing the subject's ability output and initiative), or Adaptive (primes the subject's biology for a specific modification, reducing the SS cost of the next Evolution Cipher applied to them by half). Duration varies by compound type and subject's constitution. RESOLUTION: unwilling subjects resist with an Endurance Saving Throw; compounds meter themselves — no induction reaches lethal concentration.

evolution.name: SPECIA
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, identifies and activates a Specialty Trait — a dormant or semi-expressed biological feature unique to the subject's species or lineage that has not yet expressed at full functional capacity. EFFECT — The activated Specialty Trait becomes fully functional for the duration. Examples include: heightened sensory acuity, latent venom production, limited regenerative tissue, bioluminescent communication, or pressure-resistant physiology. If the subject has no dormant traits in accessible range, the Cipher may instead enhance one already-active trait to perform at peak expression. The Curator determines available Specialty Traits based on the subject's species background.

evolution.name: INTER-SPECI
evolution.effect: Rank: Xenobiologist · Component: Two Animate Subjects. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to two animate subjects simultaneously, creates a temporary genetic bridge between them. Compatible genetic sequences are identified, isolated, and prepared for cross-species integration. The process requires at least one Genetic Model for each subject to be established before activation. EFFECT — Transfers one selected Specialty Trait or modified attribute from one subject to the other for the duration — or permanently, if a Gene Edit is applied to anchor it. The receiving subject temporarily experiences physiological stress as their biology adapts (Endurance Saving Throw required; failure indicates temporary incapacitation during integration). Incompatible species may experience cascading rejection responses if the Curator determines biological conflict — rejection expels the graft rather than the patient. Reserved exclusively for Xenobiologist-rank Inquisitors due to the procedure's extreme complexity.

evolution.name: TEMP HP
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, rapidly stimulates redundant tissue growth along high-damage-exposure areas — joints, torso, skull, and exposed muscle groups. The new tissue is not permanent; it forms over the existing structure as a biologically reactive buffer, disposable by design. EFFECT — Grants the subject temporary Hit Points equal to 2d8 + the Inquisitor's Ode Level, lasting until expended or until the scene ends. Temporary HP absorbs incoming damage before the subject's actual HP is reduced. If unused, the tissue reabsorbs harmlessly within the hour. Can be stacked with other recovery effects but cannot exceed the subject's maximum HP when combined.

evolution.name: GENETIC HERITAGE
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, reaches beyond the subject's active genome and into their evolutionary history — accessing dormant ancestral sequences that pre-date the subject's current species form, encoded but no longer expressed. EFFECT — Selectively reactivates one ancestral trait from the subject's genetic heritage for the duration. The available traits are determined by the Curator based on the subject's species lore. Examples include prehistoric endurance, environmental resistance, heightened predatory instincts, or primordial regenerative capacity. The awakened trait operates at the peak of its ancestral expression — often exceeding the power of the modern equivalent. Ancestral traits cannot be made permanent without extreme biological intervention.

evolution.name: CELLULAR MASTERY
evolution.effect: Rank: Xenobiologist · Component: Animate (self or willing). EFFECT — Full manual control of biological cellular processes in the host body for 3 rounds. Can: stop bleeding (heal 1d8 HP as free action); close wounds at accelerated rate; suppress pain (negate Wounded conditions); temporarily shut down non-essential biological processes. Extended control requires 3 SS/round and increasingly risks cellular fatigue.

evolution.name: ANCESTRAL AWAKENING
evolution.effect: Rank: Xenobiologist · Component: Animate (self). EFFECT — For 5 minutes, the Inquisitor temporarily expresses an entire lineage's accumulated biological adaptations — gaining a compilation of traits from ancestors across multiple generations.
