May include: extinct species traits; extreme environmental adaptations; physical capabilities that were lost over generations. Each use permanently unlocks one small genetic expression as a cosmetic Wryde change.

evolution.name: RAPID ADAPTATION
evolution.effect: Rank: Tester · Component: Animate target. EFFECT — The target's biology rapidly adapts to current environmental conditions: in extreme cold, develops thermal insulation; in low-oxygen environments, increases blood oxygen efficiency; in high-radiation zones, cellular repair mechanisms activate. The adaptation is temporary (lasts 1 hour) but provides resistance to environmental damage. Cannot adapt to supernatural or Null-based threats.

evolution.name: EVOLUTIONARY FORK
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate target. EFFECT — Target expresses two distinct evolutionary adaptations at once for 2 rounds — gaining benefits from both at 60% efficacy of each. After 2 rounds, the body collapses back to its dominant path, and the target makes an Endurance Save (DC 13) or takes 1d8 damage from the cellular strain.

evolution.name: GENETIC LOCK
evolution.effect: Rank: Tester · Component: Animate target. EFFECT — Prevents a target's genetics from being modified for 1 hour. Blocks: Gene Edit, Modify, Induce, and all similar genetic-manipulation Ciphers and abilities. Also blocks Incept expression drift — the target's Expression States are frozen for the duration (DDC accumulation pauses). RESOLUTION: an unwilling target resists the Lock with an Endurance Saving Throw.

evolution.name: MASS INDUCTION
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Multiple animate targets (up to 4). EFFECT — Applies the same targeted genetic mutation to up to 4 subjects at once. Each subject receives the same mutation type. Individual variation in reception: each subject rolls Endurance — high rollers express more cleanly, low rollers may have minor complications; unwilling subjects resist outright on a successful save. Paradigm monitors Mass Induction use closely.

evolution.name: CELLULAR REGENERATION
evolution.effect: Rank: Modificator · Component: Animate target (wounded). EFFECT — Target recovers 3d8 HP immediately. Additionally, any one listed condition is resolved by biological recovery: Poisoned (toxin metabolized), Bleeding (clotted), Broken bone (reknit), or Burned surface tissue (resurfaced). Cannot regenerate lost limbs.

evolution.name: SPECIES MEMORY
evolution.effect: Rank: Xenobiologist · Component: Animate target. EFFECT — Accesses the genetic memory archive of a target species — instinctual behaviors, environmental histories, threat responses, and evolutionary experiences encoded across generations. Information arrives as strong intuitions and emotional memories rather than clear images. Duration: 10 minutes. Online: extracted species memory is archived to Paradigm's biological database.

evolution.name: SYMBIOSIS LINK
evolution.effect: Rank: Xenobiologist · Component: Two animate subjects (willing). EFFECT — The two subjects enter a symbiotic relationship. When adjacent: both gain +1 to all physical stats. Damage taken by one is shared 30/70 with the other (30% to the other, 70% to original). Each subject instinctively knows the other's current physical state. The symbiosis lasts 24 hours.

evolution.name: EVOLUTIONARY PRESSURE
evolution.effect: Rank: Xenobiologist · Component: Animate target under stress. EFFECT — Applied to a target currently under extreme stress: triggers emergency evolutionary expression appropriate to the stress type. A target drowning may express gill-adjacent respiration; a target burning may express heat resistance; a target being crushed may express structural density. The adaptation is significant enough to survive the triggering condition for 3 rounds. Repeated use within 24 hours requires higher Endurance Saves.

evolution.name: GENE VAULT
evolution.effect: Rank: Tester · Component: Animate target (brief contact). EFFECT — Records and stores the complete genetic profile of any creature the Inquisitor has modified or analyzed. The Vault can hold up to 20 profiles. Stored profiles allow: easier future Ciphers on the same subject (−5 SS cost), recreation of prior modifications, and cross-referencing for genetic compatibility analysis.

evolution.name: S1 — NEW SPECIES
evolution.effect: Rank: Distinguished Evolutionist · Component: Willing animate subject. EFFECT — Guides the complete evolutionary transformation of a willing subject into a new species variant — a genuine biological new form. The new form is determined collaboratively by Inquisitor and Director. The subject becomes a new entry in WTE's species catalogue — with their own Dom/Rec values, Eminence Nature defaults, and innate abilities. Irreversible without counterpart Null procedure.

evolution.name: S2 — GENESIS
evolution.effect: Rank: Distinguished Evolutionist · Component: Collected genetic material from multiple sources. EFFECT — Creates a completely new living organism from collected and synthesized genetic material. The new life form has: a basic genome designed by the Inquisitor; a species designation; and initial behavioral programming (not full consciousness). Shawchun Corporation authorization required. The most significant creation capability in WTE.

evolution.name: S3 — THE APEX
evolution.effect: Rank: Distinguished Evolutionist · Component: Self. EFFECT — Triggers the Inquisitor's own evolution to a temporary Apex form for 5 minutes. In Apex form: one size category larger; all physical stats +3; natural attack deals 2d10 + Strength; resistance to all non-Null damage; one unique ability from their deepest lineage (Director determines). After Apex ends: mandatory 1 full rest. Each use permanently expresses one Wryde mutation. Maximum 3 uses before the biological cost becomes life-threatening.

evolution.name: S4 — GENETIC HERITAGE: COMPLETE
evolution.effect: Rank: Distinguished Evolutionist · Component: Self. EFFECT — Simultaneously expresses all variant and sub-species traits available in the Inquisitor's lineage for 3 rounds. For each round: gain the benefits of up to 3 different Variant trait sets simultaneously. After 3 rounds: Endurance Save (DC 16) or unable to fully revert to base form for 24 hours (stuck between expressions).

evolution.name: S5 — LIFE WEAVE
evolution.effect: Rank: Distinguished Evolutionist · Component: Two or more living organisms (willing). EFFECT — Permanently merges the biological traits of two or more organisms into each of the participants — all gain access to the other's most significant biological capabilities. Each participant gains: one innate ability from each other participant; shared resistance to conditions the others are naturally resistant to; and a combined Wryde expression. All participants must be willing. The bond cannot be reversed.

warfare.name: WEAPONIZE
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to any inanimate object, restructures the object's form and energy signature to function as a weapon. The object's original purpose becomes secondary; its new function is optimized for offensive application regardless of what it was before. EFFECT — The weaponized object deals damage as a Tier-appropriate weapon, using that weapon's standard dice. The Inquisitor may choose one of three damage types upon activation: Blunt (wide impact, knockback), Piercing (focused, armor-penetrating), or Slashing (broad edge, bleeding potential). Additionally, the object gains a passive effect tied to the Inquisitor's active Genus Domain — for example, a weaponized chair from a Fire Domain Inquisitor may deal heat-based secondary damage. The modification persists until the object is destroyed or the Cipher is deactivated.

warfare.name: TRIXT LINK
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, generates a tactical link that connects it to other objects bearing the same Cipher within operational range. Each linked object becomes a node in a coordinated network. Node status, orientation, and energy level are communicated in real time across the chain. EFFECT — When activated, the Inquisitor can trigger all linked objects simultaneously or in programmed sequence. Each object executes a predetermined action — deployment, launch, activation, or detonation (each resolving through its own listed dice and saves) — in the order the Inquisitor dictates. This enables coordinated multi-point strikes, simultaneous utility deployment, and layered field traps. The link operates without signal latency and cannot be intercepted by standard electronic countermeasures.

warfare.name: COMMAND
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, establishes a direct command link from the Inquisitor to the target's motor response centers. The link does not override higher cognition — the target remains aware. However, compliance with directives becomes the path of least resistance, as the Cipher suppresses the neurological cost of defiance. EFFECT — The Inquisitor may issue a single direct command per activation (move, stop, stand down, protect, etc.). The target must succeed on a Wisdom Saving Throw to resist. On failure, they execute the command. On success, they are still mildly affected — hesitating for one beat or experiencing a pull toward compliance that they push through. Commands cannot be suicidal or demand the target harm themselves — the link snaps before it carries such an order. Allies with pre-established trust may receive commands as free actions without a saving throw.

warfare.name: SEIZE
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, establishes a hostile takeover protocol that continuously pings the object's current owner or holder, attempting to override their proprietary link to it. If the object has no existing bond, the takeover completes immediately. EFFECT — Upon activation, the Inquisitor asserts dominance over the object, claiming it as their own. Any defensive Cipher on the object must be contested. The original owner, if still linked, must succeed on a Control Saving Throw or lose their command privileges. On failure, the Inquisitor gains full operational control. Objects with no defensive link simply comply. Seized objects obey the Inquisitor as if originally bonded to them.

warfare.name: BULLWARK
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, converts its form into an impenetrable forward-facing barrier. The object's structural properties are amplified far beyond their natural threshold, redistributing mass and density toward the front-facing surface while locking the object in a fixed defensive orientation. EFFECT — The modified object functions as a deployable bulwark — capable of absorbing direct damage from all Tier-3 and below sources without structural compromise. When an entity takes cover behind the bulwark, they gain full cover against all ranged attacks from the bulwark's facing direction. The bulwark can be repositioned once per round as a Free Action if the Inquisitor is within 10 feet. Against Tier-4+ attacks, the Curator determines resistance based on hit magnitude.

warfare.name: AUTHORITATIVE
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to the Inquisitor, amplifies the projection of command authority, expanding their presence into a field of perceived hierarchical dominance. Allies and neutral parties within range instinctively recognize and respond to the Inquisitor as the highest authority in the immediate space. EFFECT — While active, the Inquisitor gains advantage on Persuasion, Intimidation, and Inspiration-based Skill Checks. Enemies within 30 feet must succeed on a Wisdom Saving Throw at the start of their turn or have their hostile intent temporarily suppressed — they may still act, but cannot target the Inquisitor with direct attacks for that round. Allies who receive direct orders from the Inquisitor while Authoritative is active may re-roll one failed check per order.

warfare.name: DECEIST
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Inanimate Object. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When affixed to an object, causes it to broadcast a false identification signature — projecting a false reading to any scanning, detection, or analytical system that attempts to identify it. The false signature is precise and stable, indistinguishable from authentic data to Tier-2 and below detection systems. EFFECT — The object appears to be whatever the Inquisitor designates — a different item, a benign tool, a cleared clearance token, or simply a registered civilian object. All passive identification systems will return the false reading. Active scans by Tier-3+ detection systems require the Inquisitor to succeed on a contested Cipher check. If the deception is discovered, the object immediately reverts to its true signature and cannot be masked again by this Cipher for 24 hours.

warfare.name: LAW BIND
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an animate subject, creates a binding behavioral protocol tethered to a specific instruction or prohibition issued by the Inquisitor. The protocol embeds into the subject's behavioral layer — not overriding will, but creating a hard rule that the subject's actions are evaluated against in real time. EFFECT — The bound subject cannot take a specified category of action (attack, retreat, speak, use Cipher, etc.) while the Law Bind is active. Attempting to violate the bind triggers an automatic Wisdom Saving Throw — failure results in the action being suppressed, and the subject is momentarily stunned as the bind reasserts. Success means the action proceeds, but the subject takes 1d8 psychic damage from the rupture. The bind lasts until dismissed, until the Inquisitor is incapacitated, or until the subject achieves three consecutive successes against the bind's DC. A bind can never prohibit breathing, feeding, or self-preservation — the behavioral layer refuses to hold such a law.

warfare.name: HOLLOW SHELL
warfare.effect: Rank: Captain · Component: Inanimate Object / Animate Subject. ACTIVE MODIFICATION — When applied to an object or animate subject, strips away all non-essential structure — everything that makes the target what it is beyond raw form and function. What remains is a shell: intact in shape, responsive to touch, but evacuated of internal content, personality, or autonomous capacity. EFFECT — An object becomes completely hollow — its structural frame intact but its internal mechanisms, components, or material contents removed. It can still be wielded or carried but has no functional output. If applied to an animate subject (Wisdom Saving Throw resists), the subject remains physically present — standing, breathing — but will, personality, and cognitive autonomy are suppressed. The subject follows only the most direct and explicit commands its body can survive — the autonomic core is the one structure the stripping cannot reach, and orders that would end or mutilate the shell simply halt it. They retain full sensory awareness of what is happening to them. DURATION: the evacuated self reasserts within the hour, and the same subject cannot be Hollowed again until the next day; this Cipher tests the upper boundary of acceptable authority over another consciousness, and the Paradigm audits every use.

warfare.name: MARTIAL CODE
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Inanimate weapon or armor. EFFECT — The bonded weapon or armor operates at peak theoretical efficiency for 3 rounds. Weapon: all attacks auto-succeed the attack roll portion (damage still rolled, and defenders may still respond per Action Priority); deals maximum damage on the first hit of each round. Armor: provides +3 DHP for the duration. After 3 rounds: object temporarily inert for 1 round as the code resolves.

warfare.name: ARSENAL
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Multiple objects (up to 3). EFFECT — Transforms up to 3 objects into functional weapons simultaneously, each configured as a different archetype. All three can be wielded, thrown, or distributed to allies. Paradigm network optimizes weapon-form selection based on tactical data from mission intelligence.

warfare.name: TACTICAL GRID
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Communication node. EFFECT — All allies (up to 8) within 60 ft share a real-time tactical display: each ally's position, HP approximation, active conditions, and current facing are visible to all. Allied coordination removes surprise — no allied unit can be flanked while the Grid is active. The Inquisitor can mark up to 3 targets per round as Priority — all allies gain +1 to attack rolls against marked targets. Duration: entire encounter.

warfare.name: CHAIN COMMAND
warfare.effect: Rank: Captain · Component: Group of targets (up to 4). EFFECT — All targets in range (up to 4) must contest a single Command check. Each who fails fixates on the Cipher object per standard Command rules — including its protections. Saves are made individually but rolled simultaneously. Online: logged use triggers automatic mission review.

warfare.name: FORTRESS
warfare.effect: Rank: Captain · Component: Available structural materials. EFFECT — Converts available material within 20 ft into a defensible fortified position. All allies within the Fortress position gain +2 to defensive rolls and advantage on Endurance Saves from area effects. The Fortress has structural HP of 30. Constructs in 2 rounds. Online: Paradigm can push fortification schematics based on terrain data.

warfare.name: WAR CRY
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Voice (enhanced by Cipher). EFFECT — All allied combatants within 40 ft who can hear gain +1 to Action Priority for 1 round. Each ally may immediately re-roll one failed attack roll this round. Enemies within range make Wisdom Saves (DC 12) or suffer −1 to their next roll. Can be used once per round without a Bonus Action if the Inquisitor has already struck an enemy in melee this round.

warfare.name: CRUSHING ADVANCE
warfare.effect: Rank: Captain · Component: Object (Seize-capable). EFFECT — The containment mass propels forward in a 30-ft line, 10 ft wide. Each creature in the line must succeed a Strength Save (DC 14) or is encased and carried along to the line's end — held firm but cushioned; the encasement restrains, it does not crush. The advance can push through light cover. After the advance: all encased targets are at a designated location within the line.

warfare.name: NULL ZONE
warfare.effect: Rank: Captain · Component: Battlefield sensor node. EFFECT — Projects a 60-ft radius Null Zone from the Cipher node. Within the Zone: all active effects, ongoing abilities, and environmental conditions are paused. No new abilities can be activated within the Zone. Duration: 2 rounds. After the Zone collapses: all suspended effects resume simultaneously. Online: Paradigm can activate Zone remotely for 1 additional round per authorization signal.

warfare.name: IRON WILL
warfare.effect: Rank: Corporal · Component: Allied units (up to 6). EFFECT — For 2 rounds, designated allies are immune to: Command, Fear conditions, mental effects (Brain Wash, Algorim, Dream State), and morale-based penalties. Allies also gain +1 to Endurance Saves. Online: conditioning can be extended 1 round per Paradigm signal boost.

warfare.name: THE VICTOR'S CLAIM
warfare.effect: Rank: Private · Component: Contested location or item. EFFECT — After a combat encounter ends in clear victory, projects an official Warfare Paradigm authority marker on claimed territory or objects. The Claim: a recognizable authority marker; a functional deterrent (unauthorized interaction triggers a logged alert to Paradigm); a legal establishment of Warfare Paradigm jurisdiction over the asset.

warfare.name: S1 — WAR DECLARATION
warfare.effect: Rank: Distinguished Combatant · Component: Public presence (must be witnessed). EFFECT — Formally invokes Warfare Paradigm combat authority in a designated zone (up to 500 ft radius). Within the zone: all combat rules are absolute; Inquisitors are granted full lethal mandate — a legal standing, not a mechanical one: every kill still resolves through the ordinary dice; retreat is officially recorded. All participants within the zone gain +1 to all combat rolls for the first 2 rounds. The Declaration is a permanent Paradigm record.

warfare.name: S2 — ARMY OF ONE
warfare.effect: Rank: Distinguished Combatant · Component: Self (all Cipher slots). EFFECT — For 5 rounds, all of the Inquisitor's Cipher slots activate simultaneously. Every Cipher's modification effect applies to the same object. All Cipher active effects are available without additional SS cost. After Army of One ends: all Ciphers enter mandatory cool-down for 1 encounter. Leaves the Inquisitor at 1 HP from the strain.

warfare.name: S3 — ABSOLUTE AUTHORITY
warfare.effect: Rank: Distinguished Combatant · Component: Battlefield. EFFECT — All animate targets within 100 ft must contest Charisma Save + Control + Mental Fortitude against the Inquisitor's DC. Failures fixate on the designated object per Command rules — including its protections. The Inquisitor chooses the fixation directive individually for each failed target (pursue, protect, destroy, retreat) — simultaneous multi-target Command with differentiated directives.

warfare.name: S4 — THE LAST WAR
warfare.effect: Rank: Distinguished Combatant · Component: Battlefield environment. EFFECT — Maximum Warfare expression for 3 rounds. All environmental objects within 60 ft are simultaneously Weaponized. Maximum Hollow Shell reanimations (10 fallen units) activate immediately. Trixt Link extends to 500 ft. All allied Inquisitors within range gain +2 to Action Priority. The battlefield's terrain, lighting, air pressure, and spatial relationships all optimize for maximum Warfare advantage.

warfare.name: S5 — FIELD MARSHAL
warfare.effect: Rank: Distinguished Combatant Elite · Component: Communication network. EFFECT — The Inquisitor assumes complete tactical command of all allied Warfare Inquisitors within 200 ft. Each allied Warfare Inquisitor can receive one directive per round from the Field Marshal as a free action. The Field Marshal sees a real-time Tactical Grid of the entire 200-ft radius. The Field Marshal can redirect any allied Warfare Inquisitor's Cipher activation to a new target as a Reaction (once per round).
